reason: Architectural choice: E2E-first testing via __tests__/components/ with MSW mocks. Components not unit-tested; integration test (InspectionForm.integration.test.tsx) covers critical path. RTL unit testing impractical for 271 UI primitives; Next.js 15+ RSC guidance recommends E2E instead.
Vulnerable versions: v15 <15.2.3, v13-v14 any. Current v16.2.4 is safe.
No auth/token/session in middleware.ts or App Router. Header not filtered in code, but attack requires header injection at ingress (WAF/proxy level, not app responsibility).
next: Verify WAF rules on production proxy

issue: CSP headers allow 'unsafe-eval' + 'unsafe-inline' — XSS injection vector
impact: Cross-site scripting via inline scripts, event handlers, eval() calls

issue: useEffect with empty deps [] — confusing pattern even if functionally correct for mount-only side effects
recommendation: Add ESLint comment: // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps — mount-only side effect
rationale: React 19 Compiler auto-memoizes; empty deps can break if developer intent changes. Document intent explicitly.

impact: Reduces XSS attack surface from 'unsafe' CSP to strict nonce-based CSP

conditions: Lighthouse CI + visual regression baseline within 2w
